packageRules.description: Group each controller release bump with its CRD re-vendor. semver versioning so the metio calendar tags (YYYY.M.D = three integers) order correctly; the default versioning does not. maxMajorIncrement: 0 disables Renovate's default 500-major cap — under semver the calendar tag's year reads as the major (e.g. 2026), so the first bump from the 0.0.0 bootstrap is a +2026 major jump that the cap would otherwise silently skip.

customManagers.description: Bump each controller chart's committed appVersion from the source repo's GitHub releases. A `# renovate: datasource=github-releases depName=metio/<repo>` comment sits directly above the appVersion key (depName must match a packageRule matchPackageNames entry). Tracking the GitHub release rather than the ghcr image is deliberate: the controller release pipeline pushes the image before it creates the git tag/release, so an image tag can briefly exist with no matching git tag — and vendor-crds.sh plus the dev-chart smoke both resolve that tag, not the image. The release job needs the container job, so a release implies its image exists too, making the bump consistent (tag => image => CRDs). CRD re-vendoring is handled by .github/workflows/bump.yml on the resulting PR (hosted Renovate cannot run postUpgradeTasks). NOTE: the joi chart is deliberately NOT managed here — its per-library JOI image tags default to `latest` (the version is selected in the import path), refreshed by the jsonnet-oci-images pipeline + the OCIRepository re-pull interval + sync-joi.yml, so Renovate has no role for it.

Bump the pinned commit SHA of the shared metio/git-cliff-config repo whose cliff.toml / cliff-artifacthub.toml the release workflow loads via --config-url. Both URLs embed the same SHA, so one matcher capturing the digest updates every occurrence.
